currentState.note: The majority of inventory is non-cat (dog kennels, small animal cages, bird cages, chicken coops, agility equipment). However, the site's existing SEO collections are HEAVILY cat-focused (30+ cat-related collections vs ~15 for all other animals combined).

A_noPivot.description: Continue as general pet store with broad category coverage
pros: No risk of losing existing non-cat traffic
Larger product catalog for long-tail queries
Guinea pig cage niche remains supported
cons: Topical dilution: Google sees 684 products across 15+ animal types
No category authority — competing with Chewy, Amazon, PetSmart on every front
Crawl budget wasted on 420 'OTHER' products with weak ranking potential
Brand identity is unclear to users and search engines
A_noPivot.expectedOutcome: Slow, incremental growth. Unlikely to reach page 1 for any competitive term within 12 months.

B_indoorCatFocus.description: Reposition as Indoor Cat Authority: Cat Trees (primary), Litter Boxes (secondary), Cat Wall Systems (tertiary). Non-cat products remain but are de-prioritized.
pros: Strong topical authority signal to Google for 'indoor cat' cluster
64 cat trees + 44 litter products = sufficient depth for silo architecture
20+ existing collections already target these queries
Higher AOV in cat trees ($162 avg) vs general products ($130 avg)
Clear brand differentiation from generalist competitors
cons: Some existing traffic from dog/small animal queries may see reduced internal linking
Only 2 self-cleaning litter boxes — cannot build a full primary silo around this
Only 3 wall systems — cannot be a standalone differentiation pillar
Guinea pig cage niche (current Tier A secondary) gets less prominence
B_indoorCatFocus.expectedOutcome: Measurable ranking improvement for cat tree/litter queries within 60-90 days. Page 1 positions for 5-10 long-tail cat tree queries within 6 months.

recommendation.rationale: Indoor cat focus is correct, but the original 3-pillar structure must be adjusted based on actual inventory:

adjustedPillars.role: SECONDARY — Broader litter category including enclosures, furniture-style, and the 2 self-cleaning units
adjustedPillars.viability: GOOD — Sufficient depth when combining all litter types

adjustedPillars.role: TERTIARY — Catios, wall systems, beds, tunnels, scratchers combined into one supporting pillar
adjustedPillars.viability: ADEQUATE — Enough for supporting content but not standalone authority

doNOT: Do NOT build a dedicated 'Self-Cleaning Litter Box' primary hub with only 2 products — it will look thin and hurt trust
Do NOT build a standalone 'Cat Wall Systems' pillar with only 3 products
Do NOT delete or noindex non-cat products — they still capture long-tail traffic

riskAssessment.shortTermRankingFluctuation: Minimal. We're reorganizing internal links and navigation, not removing content. Existing URLs remain intact.
riskAssessment.ninetyDayVisibilityShift: +15-25% for cat tree queries, +10-15% for litter queries. Non-cat queries: -5% max (reduced internal linking only).
mitigations: Keep all existing URLs and products active
Maintain guinea pig cage collections as Tier A secondary
Only change: navigation prominence, internal link density, and homepage hero content
Non-cat products stay indexed at current tier level
